content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Sounds good. I'll mirror that shape: in parse()'s while-loop, add an elif branch detecting 'domain' param name, parsing a string-literal value into a local 'domain: t.Optional[str] = None', raising TemplateAssertionError on duplicate or non-string-literal. In _make_node() I add a trailing 'domain: t.Optional[str] = None' parameter, and when building the gettext/ngettext call nodes I swap to dgettext/dngettext(domain, ...) when domain is set. I'll also add dgettext/dngettext to _install_callables()' GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS-style globals wiring. No touches to your metadata/note/created/modified logic. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Implementation complete: added domain= parameter support to trans tag. Diff touches: (1) GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS gains dgettext/dngettext, (2) new _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext newstyle wrappers, (3) parse() while-loop: comma-gate changed to 'if variables or domain is not None:', added handling inside the assign branch for token.value == 'domain' (parses string-literal, raises TemplateAssertionError on duplicate or non-literal), (4) _make_node() gained trailing 'domain' param and emits dgettext/dngettext Call nodes instead of gettext/ngettext when domain is set, (5) _install/_install_null/_install_callables wire dgettext/dngettext into environment globals, with null-translations providing domain-agnostic passthroughs. All 830 existing tests pass. Patch written to patch.txt in my sandbox.
